ai_agnostic_hint: Target http://localhost:8080/v1 for OpenAI-compatible FOSS API access.

entries.title: default.images.toml
entries.full_content:
# MiOS OCI Image Configuration
# This is a TEMPLATE - copy to $HOME/.config/mios/images.toml and customize
#
# Location: /usr/share/mios/config/default.images.toml (in image)
# User copy: $HOME/.config/mios/images.toml (overrides these defaults)

[base]
# Base image for MiOS builds
# Options:
#   - ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable-nvidia (default - NVIDIA GPU support)
#   - ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable (Intel/AMD GPUs)
#   - ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore:stable (minimal, no HCI)
image = "ghcr.io/ublue-os/ucore-hci:stable-nvidia"

# Base image variant (if needed for multi-arch)
# Options: "", "stable", "latest", "40", "41"
variant = "stable-nvidia"

[builder]
# Bootc Image Builder (BIB) image
image = "quay.io/centos-bootc/bootc-image-builder:latest"

# BIB variant/tag
variant = "latest"

[output]
# Output image name (local builds)
name = "localhost/mios"

# Output image tags (multiple tags supported)
tags = ["latest", "v0.1.3"]

# Registry for pushed images (if pushing)
registry = "ghcr.io"

# Repository path (for pushing)
# Example: "your-username/mios" → ghcr.io/your-username/mios:latest
repository = "Kabuki94/MiOS-bootstrap"

[registry]
# Default container registry
default = "ghcr.io"

# Automatically push after successful build
push_on_build = false

# Use authenticated registries (reads from $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mios/credentials/registry-auth.json)
use_auth = false

# Verify TLS certificates
tls_verify = true

[mirrors]
# Container registry mirrors (for offline/airgap environments)
# Format: "original" = "mirror"
# Example:
# "ghcr.io" = "registry.local:5000"
enabled = false

[mirrors.map]
# Add your mirrors here
# "ghcr.io" = "your-mirror.com"

[pull]
# Image pull policy
# Options: "always", "missing", "never"
policy = "missing"

# Pull timeout (seconds)
timeout = 600

# Retry failed pulls
retry = 3

entries.title: default.build.toml
entries.full_content:
# MiOS Build Configuration
# This is a TEMPLATE - copy to $HOME/.config/mios/build.toml and customize
#
# Location: /usr/share/mios/config/default.build.toml (in image)
# User copy: $HOME/.config/mios/build.toml (overrides these defaults)

[artifacts]
# Which artifacts to generate
# Options: "qcow2", "raw", "iso", "vmdk", "vhdx", "ami", "hyperv"
enabled = ["qcow2"]

# Output directory for artifacts
output_dir = "./output"

[qcow2]
# QEMU QCOW2 image settings
enabled = true
disk_size = "50G"
format = "qcow2"
compression = true

[raw]
# RAW disk image settings
enabled = false
disk_size = "50G"
format = "raw"

[iso]
# Anaconda installer ISO settings
enabled = false
installer_type = "anaconda"
include_netinstall = true
# Minimum ISO size optimization
minsize = true

[vmdk]
# VMware VMDK image settings
enabled = false
disk_size = "50G"
format = "vmdk"
compatibility = "vmware"

[vhdx]
# Hyper-V VHDX image settings
enabled = false
disk_size = "50G"
format = "vhdx"

[ami]
# AWS AMI image settings
enabled = false
disk_size = "50G"
format = "raw"

[hyperv]
# Hyper-V image settings
enabled = false
disk_size = "50G"

[flatpaks]
# Flatpak application installation
enabled = false

# Path to flatpaks.list (one app per line)
# Default: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mios/flatpaks.list
source_file = ""

# Flatpak remote to use
remote = "flathub"

# Install system-wide (true) or user-local (false)
system_wide = false

[nvidia]
# NVIDIA GPU support
# Note: Only relevant if base image is ucore-hci:stable-nvidia
enabled = true

# Driver version ("latest" or specific version like "550.120")
driver_version = "latest"

# Enable CUDA support
cuda = true

# Enable NVENC/NVDEC (video encoding/decoding)
video_codec = true

[amd]
# AMD GPU support (ROCm)
enabled = false

# ROCm version
rocm_version = "6.0"

# Enable ROCm for compute
compute = false

[intel]
# Intel GPU support
enabled = false

# Enable Intel Media Driver
media_driver = true

# Enable Intel Compute Runtime
compute_runtime = true

[kernel]
# Kernel configuration
# Note: Base image ships with ucore-hci kernel; these are overlays

# Additional kernel modules to load
modules = []

# Kernel parameters to add
# Example: ["quiet", "splash", "nvidia-drm.modeset=1"]
parameters = []

[bootloader]
# Bootloader configuration (GRUB2)

# Bootloader timeout (seconds)
timeout = 5

# Show boot menu
show_menu = true

# Default boot entry (0 = first entry)
default_entry = 0

[selinux]
# SELinux configuration
# Options: "enforcing", "permissive", "disabled"
mode = "enforcing"

# SELinux policy type
# Options: "targeted", "mls"
policy = "targeted"

[firewall]
# Firewall configuration (firewalld)
enabled = true

# Default zone
default_zone = "public"

# Open ports (format: "port/protocol")
# Example: ["22/tcp", "80/tcp", "443/tcp"]
open_ports = []

# Open services (firewalld service names)
# Example: ["ssh", "http", "https"]
open_services = ["ssh"]

[ssh]
# SSH server configuration
enabled = true

# Allow root login
# Options: "yes", "no", "prohibit-password"
root_login = "prohibit-password"

# Allow password authentication
password_auth = false

# SSH port
port = 22

[users]
# Default user configuration (used during build)

# Default username (can be overridden by installer)
default_user = "mios"

# Create default user during build
create_default_user = false

# Default user groups
# Example: ["wheel", "docker", "libvirt"]
default_groups = ["wheel"]

[passwords]
# Password hash for default user (SHA-512 crypt format)
# Generate with: mkpasswd -m sha-512
# Leave empty to disable password login
default_user_hash = ""

# Require password change on first login
force_password_change = false

[hostname]
# Default hostname (can be overridden by installer)
default = "mios"

[timezone]
# Default timezone
# Example: "America/New_York", "Europe/London", "UTC"
default = "UTC"

[locale]
# Default locale
# Example: "en_US.UTF-8", "en_GB.UTF-8"
default = "en_US.UTF-8"

# Additional locales to generate
additional = []

[keyboard]
# Default keyboard layout
# Example: "us", "uk", "de", "fr"
layout = "us"

# Keyboard variant (optional)
variant = ""

[signing]
# Secure Boot signing configuration

# Enable Secure Boot signing
enabled = false

# MOK (Machine Owner Key) private key path
# Path: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mios/credentials/mok-key.priv
mok_key = ""

# MOK certificate path
# Path: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mios/credentials/mok-cert.pem
mok_cert = ""

[rechunk]
# OSTree rechunk configuration (composefs optimization)

# Enable rechunking after build
enabled = false

# Rechunk labels (for OCI image layer optimization)
labels = []

entries.title: MiOS Configuration Templates

# MiOS Configuration Templates

## 📁 Template Files

- `flatpaks.list` - Flatpak applications template

entries.full_content:
# MiOS Configuration Templates

This directory contains **system default configuration templates** that ship with MiOS.

## 📁 Template Files

- `default.env.toml` - Environment configuration template
- `default.images.toml` - OCI image references template
- `default.build.toml` - Build configuration template
- `flatpaks.list` - Flatpak applications template

## 🏠 User Configuration Location

**DO NOT EDIT THESE FILES DIRECTLY!**

These templates are read-only system defaults. User configurations belong in:

```
$HOME/.config/mios/
├── env.toml           # Your environment config
├── images.toml        # Your OCI image preferences
├── build.toml         # Your build configuration
├── flatpaks.list      # Your Flatpak applications
└── credentials/       # Your credentials (never committed)
```

## 🚀 First-Time Setup

Initialize your user-space configuration:

```bash
# Run the initialization script
just init-user-space

# Or manually
./tools/init-user-space.sh
```

This will:
1. Create `$HOME/.config/mios/` directory structure
2. Copy these templates to your user config
3. Set up credentials directory with `.gitignore`
4. Create XDG Base Directory structure

## 📝 Customizing Configuration

After initialization, edit your user configuration files:

```bash
# Edit environment configuration
$EDITOR ~/.config/mios/env.toml

# Edit OCI image preferences
$EDITOR ~/.config/mios/images.toml

# Edit build configuration
$EDITOR ~/.config/mios/build.toml

# Add Flatpak applications
$EDITOR ~/.config/mios/flatpaks.list
```

## 🔐 Adding Credentials

Store sensitive data in the credentials directory:

```bash
# GitHub Personal Access Token
echo "ghp_your_token_here" > ~/.config/mios/credentials/github-token
chmod 600 ~/.config/mios/credentials/github-token

# Container registry authentication (Podman/Docker format)
podman login ghcr.io
cp ~/.config/containers/auth.json ~/.config/mios/credentials/registry-auth.json

# SSH keys for private repositories
cp ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 ~/.config/mios/credentials/ssh-keys/
chmod 600 ~/.config/mios/credentials/ssh-keys/*
```

**IMPORTANT:** The credentials directory is automatically `.gitignore`'d and will **never** be committed to version control.

## 🔄 Configuration Priority

Variables are loaded in priority order (later overrides earlier):

1. **System Defaults** (these templates) - Read-only, shipped with MiOS
2. **User Configuration** (`~/.config/mios/*.toml`) - Your overrides
3. **Environment Variables** (e.g., `MIOS_BASE_IMAGE`) - Shell environment
4. **Command-Line Arguments** - Direct overrides (highest priority)

## 🌐 XDG Base Directory Compliance

MiOS follows the [XDG Base Directory Specification](https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/basedir-spec-latest.html):

| Purpose | Default Path | Environment Variable |
|---------|--------------|---------------------|
| Configuration | `~/.config/mios/` | `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mios/` |
| Data | `~/.local/share/mios/` | `$XDG_DATA_HOME/mios/` |
| Cache | `~/.cache/mios/` | `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/mios/` |
| State (logs) | `~/.local/state/mios/` | `$XDG_STATE_HOME/mios/` |
| Runtime | `/run/user/$UID/mios/` | `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/mios/` |

## 📚 Documentation

For more information, see:
- [User-Space Separation Spec](../../specs/engineering/2026-04-27-Artifact-ENG-008-UserSpace-Separation.md)
- [Self-Build Guide](../../SELF-BUILD.md)
- [AI Agent Guide](../../AI-AGENT-GUIDE.md)

---

**Location in Image:** `/usr/share/mios/config/`
**Repository Location:** `etc/mios/templates/`

entries.title: default.env.toml
entries.full_content:
# MiOS Default Environment Configuration
# This is a TEMPLATE - copy to $HOME/.config/mios/env.toml and customize
#
# Location: /usr/share/mios/config/default.env.toml (in image)
# User copy: $HOME/.config/mios/env.toml (overrides these defaults)
#
# XDG Base Directory variables are automatically available:
#   XDG_CONFIG_HOME = $HOME/.config
#   XDG_DATA_HOME   = $HOME/.local/share
#   XDG_CACHE_HOME  = $HOME/.cache
#   XDG_STATE_HOME  = $HOME/.local/state

[mios]
version = "v0.1.3"
# User name (override this in your user config)
user = "mios"
# Hostname (override this in your user config)
hostname = "mios"

[build]
# Disable build cache (--no-cache flag)
no_cache = false
# Number of parallel build jobs
parallel_jobs = 4
# Verbose build output
verbose = false
# Build platform (auto-detected if not set)
platform = ""

[logging]
# Where to store user build logs
# Default: $XDG_STATE_HOME/mios/logs
log_dir = ""
# How many days to retain logs (0 = keep all)
retain_days = 30
# Log level: debug, info, warn, error
log_level = "info"

[paths]
# User-specific paths (use XDG variables)
config_dir = ""   # Auto: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mios
data_dir = ""     # Auto: $XDG_DATA_HOME/mios
cache_dir = ""    # Auto: $XDG_CACHE_HOME/mios
state_dir = ""    # Auto: $XDG_STATE_HOME/mios
runtime_dir = ""  # Auto: $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/mios

[preferences]
# Editor preference (used for interactive editing)
editor = "vim"
# Shell preference
shell = "bash"
# Terminal preference
terminal = "gnome-terminal"
